## [CHUNK: rule -- COMBAT]

Combat in the Age of Dragons is decisive and dangerous. Battles are resolved through opposed melee/ranged/magic checks, with morale and exhaustion playing out over the course of an engagement.

# triggers: combat, attack, melee, ranged
# priority: 7

### Melee

When two combatants engage in melee, each rolls 1d20 + combat modifier. The higher roll deals damage equal to the difference; the loser loses 1d6 HP per point of margin.

### Ranged

Ranged attacks are resolved as 1d20 + accuracy. Cover grants +2 to the defender's roll. Moving before shooting imposes -2.

### Magic in Combat

Casting a spell in combat takes 1 full action. Concentration spells last until the caster takes damage or ends them. Interrupting a caster's incantation requires a successful opposed roll.

### Morale and Retreat

When half of a unit's HP is depleted, roll a morale check (1d20 + morale modifier). On a failure, the unit routes unless a leader is present and succeeds at a rallying check.
